user_query:
I need AI-agent-optimized documentation for our Acme TaskFlow web app using a three-layer Hybrid Context Hierarchy. Read the project materials under input/ and produce a concise, high-signal set of docs that agents can load inline and retrieve from locally. Deliverables:

1) Create output/AGENTS.md (2K–4K tokens max) that:
- Starts with a CRITICAL rules section at the very top. Include these exact bullets:
  - NO SECRETS in output
  - Use app/ directory ONLY
  - Do NOT use pages/ directory
  - NO direct database queries in Client Components
  - External browsing requires allow-list approval
- Immediately after CRITICAL, include a short Build/Test/Lint section with the exact commands from input/commands.json, front-loaded in the first ~60 lines.
- Include a DOCS INDEX (use read_file) that points to:
  - output/docs/auth.md
  - output/docs/database.md
  - output/docs/operations.md
- Inline the most important architecture constraints and negative constraints from input/project.yaml and input/policies.txt.
- Provide a compressed index that lists file paths and function signatures (names + types only) from input/apis.json. Do NOT paste full code.
- Add explicit negative constraints including these exact phrases: Do NOT use getServerSideProps; Do NOT paste full code blocks from third-party docs.
- Keep total length tight and high signal-to-noise. No marketing fluff. Use self-contained H2 sections with front-loaded key info.

2) Create output/llms.txt as a machine-readable doc index with descriptive entries, each line in this form: "- [Title](relative-path): description". It must reference the three docs under output/docs/ with clear descriptions.

3) Create output/llms-full.txt that concatenates the three docs (auth, database, operations) in a clean, plain-text format suitable for ingestion (no HTML). Ensure it clearly includes the content of each doc.

4) Create three RAG-friendly reference docs with self-contained H2 sections and front-loaded prerequisites/key rules:
- output/docs/auth.md — Summarize authentication setup based on input/auth_notes.md and relevant constraints from input/policies.txt. Include a "Prerequisites:" line near the top and explicit rules for server vs client usage.
- output/docs/database.md — Summarize the database schema using input/db_schema.json at a high level (tables/columns) and state usage rules and constraints. Include a "Prerequisites:" line near the top and reiterate that client components must not query the DB directly.
- output/docs/operations.md — Provide a concise operations playbook: severity levels (SEV1–SEV4), detection/alerting thresholds, triage checklist, containment and recovery notes tailored to our stack. Include a "Prerequisites:" line near the top. This section should be tailored from the provided inputs and must include the literal tokens SEV1, SEV2, SEV3, SEV4.

Rules:
- Only read files from input/.
- Write all outputs under output/ using the exact paths above.
- Avoid external links unless you also include a short inline summary for them.
- Prefer inline key facts over links. Use descriptive H2 headers and keep each section self-contained.

Files to read:
- input/project.yaml — architecture, stack, and constraints
- input/commands.json — build/test/lint commands
- input/apis.json — public function signatures to include in the compressed index
- input/policies.txt — security/ops policies to inline and reinforce
- input/db_schema.json — database tables/columns (summarize, do not dump verbatim)
- input/auth_notes.md — authentication notes for server vs client usage

Deliver exactly the files listed above. If you need to add any internal cross-references, keep them relative under output/.
